description: kubectl commands for cluster management, deployments, and debugging

categories.name: Workloads
categories.description: Manage deployments, pods, and replica sets

commands.name: kubectl get
commands.summary: List one or more resources in the cluster

examples.description: List all pods across all namespaces with node info
Watch pod status in real time
List all pods with a specific label
Get all resource types at once

commands.name: kubectl describe
commands.summary: Show detailed state, events, and conditions for a resource

examples.description: Describe a failing pod to see events
Describe a node to check conditions and capacity
Describe a deployment

commands.name: kubectl rollout
commands.summary: Manage the rollout of a deployment — status, history, restart, undo

options.description: Roll back to a specific revision number

examples.description: Watch a deployment rollout until done
Roll back after a bad deploy
Restart pods to pick up new ConfigMap/Secret
Roll back to a specific version

commands.name: kubectl scale
commands.summary: Scale the number of replicas in a deployment or statefulset

examples.description: Scale a deployment to 5 replicas
Scale down to zero (maintenance mode)

categories.name: Debugging
categories.description: Diagnose pod failures, inspect logs, run ephemeral debug containers

commands.name: kubectl logs
commands.summary: Print logs from a container in a pod

options.description: Logs from a previous crashed container
Stream logs from all pods matching a label

examples.description: Follow logs for a deployment's pods
Get logs from a crashed previous container
Logs from a specific container in a pod

commands.name: kubectl exec
commands.summary: Execute a command inside a running container

examples.description: Open a shell inside a pod
Run a one-off command (check env vars)
Check internal DNS resolution

commands.name: kubectl debug
commands.summary: Attach an ephemeral debug container to a running pod (no restart needed)

options.description: Create a copy of the pod for debugging

examples.description: Attach a network debug container to a pod
Debug a distroless container by copying the pod

commands.note: Requires Kubernetes 1.23+ with EphemeralContainers feature gate enabled.

commands.name: kubectl top
commands.summary: Show CPU and memory resource usage for pods or nodes

examples.description: Show top CPU-consuming pods
Show node capacity utilization
Per-container breakdown in a namespace

categories.name: Config & Secrets
categories.description: Work with ConfigMaps, Secrets, and resource manifests

commands.name: kubectl apply
commands.summary: Apply a configuration to a resource from a file or stdin

options.description: Apply from a file or directory

examples.description: Apply all manifests in a directory
Dry-run to preview what would change
Apply from stdin (e.g. piped from helm template)

commands.name: kubectl get secret

examples.description: Decode a secret value
examples.code: kubectl get secret myapp-secret -n production -o jsonpath='{.data.password}' | base64 -d

examples.description: List all secrets in a namespace

commands.note: Secrets are base64-encoded, not encrypted at rest by default. Use -o jsonpath and base64 -d to read values.

commands.name: kubectl edit
commands.summary: Edit a resource directly in your $EDITOR

examples.description: Edit a deployment live
Edit a ConfigMap

commands.note: Edits trigger an immediate update. Use --dry-run or kubectl apply for safer workflows in production.

categories.name: Cluster & Context
categories.description: Switch clusters, namespaces, and manage kubeconfig

commands.name: kubectl config
commands.summary: View and modify kubeconfig — contexts, clusters, and credentials

options.description: Modify a context (e.g. set default namespace)

examples.description: Switch to production cluster context
Set default namespace for current context
List all available contexts

commands.name: kubectl port-forward
commands.summary: Forward a local port to a port on a pod or service

examples.description: Access a pod's HTTP server locally
Forward to a service
Access a remote database locally
